RUN pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir transformers bitsandbytes

# Create directory for user nobody in SAP AI Core run-time
RUN sudo mkdir -p /nonexistent/ludwig/results && \
    sudo mkdir -p /nonexistent/ludwig/data && \
    sudo mkdir -p /nonexistent/matplotlib-conf && \
    sudo mkdir -p /nonexistent/hf-home && \
    sudo mkdir -p /home/ray/ray_results && \
    sudo mkdir -p /home/ray/.triton && \
    sudo mkdir -p /home/ray/.cache && \
    sudo chown -R nobody:nogroup /nonexistent /home/ray/ray_results /home/ray/.triton /home/ray/.cache && \
    sudo chmod -R 770 /nonexistent /home/ray/ray_results /home/ray/.triton /home/ray/.cache

# Copy adapted ai_core_serve.py for SAP AI Core serving
COPY ai_core_serve.py /nonexistent/ludwig/ai_core_serve.py
ENV HF_HOME=/nonexistent/hf-home
ENV MPLCONFIGDIR=/nonexistent/matplotlib-conf

 

 

Build and push your own docker image to docker hub with commands below:

 

# 1.Login to docker hub
docker login -u <YOUR_DOCKER_USER> -p <YOUR_DOCKER_ACCESS_TOKEN>

# 2.Build the docker image
docker build --platform=linux/amd64 -t docker.io/<YOUR_DOCKER_USER>/ludwig-ray-gpu:ai-core .

# 3.Push the docker image to docker hub
docker push docker.io/<YOUR_DOCKER_USER>/ludwig-ray-gpu:ai-core

 

Once the docker image is pushed, note down the docker image uri: docker.io/<YOUR_DOCKER_USER>/ludwig-ray-gpu:ai-core, which will be used in create the configurations in SAP AI Core later.

Step 3: Dataset and Model Management in Cloud Object Storage

As mentioned above, ludwig can have direct access to cloud storage for Dataset and Model, therefore, it is not required to register Dataset and Model artifacts in SAP AI Core. For the book-genre-classification use case, I have created a S3 bucket named ludwig-custom-models, and have created the folder structure as below:

 

 

ludwgi-custom-models (s3 bucket)
    /book-genre-classification
        /config
            -config_parallel_cnn.yaml
            -config_distilbert.yaml           
        /data
            -dataset.csv
        /results

 

Step 4: Create a github repo for hosting the templates below.
1). ludwig-cli Workflow Template

Here is the yaml file about ludwig-cli-template.yaml for general workflow of running ludwig command with direct access to cloud object storage. Please replace the place holder  <REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_DOCKER_SECRET>.

 

apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: WorkflowTemplate
metadata:
  name: ludwig-cli
  annotations:
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/description: "General ludwig cli workflow with direct access to cloud storage"
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/name: "ludwig-cli"
    executables.ai.sap.com/description: "ludwig cli"
    executables.ai.sap.com/name: "ludwig-cli"
  labels:
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/id: "ludwig-cli"
    executables.ai.sap.com/id: "ludwig-cli"
    ai.sap.com/version: "1.0.0"
spec:
  imagePullSecrets:
    - name: <REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_DOCKER_SECRET>
  entrypoint: ludwig-cli
  arguments:
    parameters:
      - name: image
        value: "docker.io/ludwigai/ludwig-ray-gpu:master"
        description: "Your custom docker image for ludwig."
      - name: exportCommand
        value: "AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=<YOUR_AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID> AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=<YOUR_AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY>"
        description: "Setup environment viable(s) with export command: export <exportCommand>. For example, environment viable for cloud storage: https://ludwig.ai/latest/user_guide/cloud_storage/"
      - name: ludwigCommand
        value: "experiment --config s3://<YOUR-S3-BUCKET/PATH/TO/YOUR_CONFIG>.yaml --dataset s3://<YOUR-S3-BUCKET/PATH/TO/YOUR_DATASET>.csv --experiment_name <YOUR_EXPERIMENT_NAME> --model_name <YOUR_MODEL_NAME> --logging_level info"
        description: "Setup environment viable(s) with export command: export <exportCommand>. For example, environment viable for cloud storage: https://ludwig.ai/latest/user_guide/cloud_storage/, or environment viable HF_TOKEN for hugging face if you need to finetune a gated model from hugging face"
      - name: resourcePlan
        value: "starter"
        description: "Resource Plan of SAP AI Core. More detail available here: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/sap-ai-core-service-guide/choose-resource-plan-c58d4e584a5b40a2992265beb9b6be3c"
  templates:
    - name: ludwig-cli
      metadata:
        labels:
          ai.sap.com/resourcePlan: "{{workflow.parameters.resourcePlan}}"
      container:
        image: "{{workflow.parameters.image}}"
        imagePullPolicy: Always
        command: ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
        args:
          - >
            set -e && 
            echo "---Start Ludwig Cli Command---" && 
            export {{workflow.parameters.exportCommand}} &&
            ludwig {{workflow.parameters.ludwigCommand}} &&
            echo "---End Ludwig Cli Command---"

 

2).ludwig-serve Serving Template

Option 1:  ludwig serve with direct model access in cloud storage, no Model artifact registration in SAP AI Core. This option is used in rest of steps. Please replace the place holder  <REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_DOCKER_SECRET>.

 

apiVersion: ai.sap.com/v1alpha1
kind: ServingTemplate
metadata:
  name: ludwig-serve
  annotations:
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/description: "ludwig serve on SAP AI Core"
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/name: "ludwig-serve"
    executables.ai.sap.com/description: "ludwig serve on SAP AI Core"
    executables.ai.sap.com/name: "ludwig-serve"
  labels:
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/id: "ludwig-serve"
    ai.sap.com/version: "0.0.1"
spec:
  inputs:
    parameters:
    - name: image
      type: "string"
      default: "docker.io/ludwigai/ludwig-ray-gpu:master"
      description: "Define the url of the Docker image of which you have built for ludwig."
    - name: exportCommand
      type: "string"      
      default: "AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=<YOUR_AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID> AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=<YOUR_AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY>"
      description: "Setup environment viable(s) with export command: export <exportCommand>. For example, environment viable for cloud storage: https://ludwig.ai/latest/user_guide/cloud_storage/"
    - name: modelName
      type: "string"
      default: "my-finetune-model"
      description: "Only relevant for llm finetune models compatible with SAP Generative AI Hub SDK"
    - name: modelPath
      type: "string"
      default: "s3://<YOUR-S3-BUCKET/PATH/TO/YOUR_EXPERIMENT_RUN/model"
      description: "The target model path. Local path as /mnt/models or model path in cloud storage. https://ludwig.ai/latest/user_guide/cloud_storage/"
    - name: loggingLevel
      type: "string"
      default: "info"
      description: "logging level to be used to serve with ludwig serve --logging_level"
    - name: resourcePlan
      type: "string"
      default: "starter"
      description: "Resource plans are used to select resources in workflow and serving templates."
    - name: minReplicas
      type: "string"
      default: "1"
      description: "The lower limit for the number of replicas to which the autoscaler can scale down."
    - name: maxReplicas
      type: "string"
      default: "1"
      description: "The upper limit for the number of replicas to which the autoscaler can scale down."
  template:
    apiVersion: "serving.kserve.io/v1beta1"
    metadata:
      annotations: |
        autoscaling.knative.dev/metric: concurrency
        autoscaling.knative.dev/target: 1
        autoscaling.knative.dev/targetBurstCapacity: -1
        autoscaling.knative.dev/window: "10m"
        autoscaling.knative.dev/scaleToZeroPodRetentionPeriod: "10m"
      labels: |
        ai.sap.com/resourcePlan: "{{inputs.parameters.resourcePlan}}"
    spec: |
      predictor:
        imagePullSecrets:
        - name: <REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_DOCKER_SECRET>
        minReplicas: {{inputs.parameters.minReplicas}}
        maxReplicas: {{inputs.parameters.maxReplicas}}
        containers:
        - name: kserve-container
          image: "{{inputs.parameters.image}}"
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8000
              protocol: TCP
          command: ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
          args:
            - >
              set -e && 
              echo "-------------Starting ludwig serve--------------" &&
              export {{inputs.parameters.exportCommand}} &&
              python /nonexistent/ludwig/ai_core_serve.py
              --model_path={{inputs.parameters.modelPath}}
              --logging_level={{inputs.parameters.loggingLevel}}

 

Option 2:  Model artifact registration in SAP AI Core. 
You need to register the model artifact by manual as model output artifact is not defined in training with ludwig-cli template.

 

apiVersion: ai.sap.com/v1alpha1
kind: ServingTemplate
metadata:
  name: ludwig-serve
  annotations:
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/description: "ludwig serve on SAP AI Core"
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/name: "ludwig-serve"
    executables.ai.sap.com/description: "ludwig serve on SAP AI Core"
    executables.ai.sap.com/name: "ludwig-serve"
  labels:
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/id: "ludwig-serve"
    ai.sap.com/version: "0.0.1"
spec:
  inputs:
    artifacts:
      - name: customModel
    parameters:
    - name: image
      type: "string"
      default: "docker.io/ludwigai/ludwig-ray-gpu:master"
      description: "Define the url of the Docker image of which you have built for ludwig."
    - name: exportCommand
      type: "string"      
      default: "AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=<YOUR_AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID> AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=<YOUR_AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY>"
      description: "Setup environment viable(s) with export command: export <exportCommand>. For example, environment viable for cloud storage: https://ludwig.ai/latest/user_guide/cloud_storage/"
    - name: modelName
      type: "string"
      default: "my-finetune-model"
      description: "Only relevant for llm finetune models compatible with SAP Generative AI Hub SDK"
    - name: modelPath
      type: "string"
      default: "/mnt/models"
      description: "The target model path. Local path as /mnt/models or model path in cloud storage. https://ludwig.ai/latest/user_guide/cloud_storage/"
    - name: loggingLevel
      type: "string"
      default: "info"
      description: "logging level to be used to serve with ludwig serve --logging_level"
    - name: resourcePlan
      type: "string"
      default: "starter"
      description: "Resource plans are used to select resources in workflow and serving templates."
    - name: minReplicas
      type: "string"
      default: "1"
      description: "The lower limit for the number of replicas to which the autoscaler can scale down."
    - name: maxReplicas
      type: "string"
      default: "1"
      description: "The upper limit for the number of replicas to which the autoscaler can scale down."
  template:
    apiVersion: "serving.kserve.io/v1beta1"
    metadata:
      annotations: |
        autoscaling.knative.dev/metric: concurrency
        autoscaling.knative.dev/target: 1
        autoscaling.knative.dev/targetBurstCapacity: -1
        autoscaling.knative.dev/window: "10m"
        autoscaling.knative.dev/scaleToZeroPodRetentionPeriod: "10m"
      labels: |
        ai.sap.com/resourcePlan: "{{inputs.parameters.resourcePlan}}"
    spec: |
      predictor:
        imagePullSecrets:
        - name: <REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_DOCKER_SECRET>
        minReplicas: {{inputs.parameters.minReplicas}}
        maxReplicas: {{inputs.parameters.maxReplicas}}
        containers:
        - name: kserve-container
          image: "{{inputs.parameters.image}}"
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8000
              protocol: TCP
          command: ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
          args:
            - >
              set -e && 
              echo "-------------Starting ludwig serve--------------" &&
              export {{inputs.parameters.exportCommand}} &&
              python /nonexistent/ludwig/ai_core_serve.py
              --model_path={{inputs.parameters.modelPath}}
              --logging_level={{inputs.parameters.loggingLevel}}
          env:
            - name: STORAGE_URI
              value: "{{inputs.artifacts.customModel}}"

 

Step 5: Onboard your github repo to your SAP AI Core

Please follow this document to add a git repo through SAP AI Launchpad.

Step 6: Create an Application and Synchronize with github repo

Please follow this document to create an application  through SAP AI Launchpad.

Step 7: Create a configuration for model experiment(Training, Testing and Evaluation)

resourcePlan: If you are working with free plan of SAP AI Core, then the only option is starter, which will take around 20 hours to complete the experiment. Otherwise, you may choose infer.s or train.l with GPU acceleration, which takes around 30 mins.

ludwigCommand: experiment --config s3://ludwig-custom-models/book-genre-classification/config/config_parallel_cnn.yaml --dataset s3://ludwig-custom-models/book-genre-classification/data/dataset.csv --experiment_name parallel_cnn --model_name bgc --output_directory  s3://ludwig-custom-models/book-genre-classification/results/

Please follow this document to create a configuration for ludwig-cli scenario through SAP AI Launchpad for more detail.

Step 8: Create an execution

Create an execution for configuration in step 7 by click "Click Execution" button in last screenshot. Then you can check the logs of execution. Please follow this document to create an execution through SAP AI Launchpad.

Once the execution is completed. It will produce the result in s3://ludwig-custom-models/book-genre-classification/results/parallel_cnn_bgc/model/ as screenshot below. The three files highlighted in red will be used to serve the inference API in next steps.

Step 9: Create a configuration of ludwig-serve serving template


resourcePlan: As it is a small model, so starter is enough for test.
modelPath: Please assure the right modelPath as the output model path in step 7.

Step 10: Create a deployment for serving

Create a deployment for configuration in step 9 by clicking "Click Deployment" button in last screenshot, note down the deployment id to be used later. Then you can check the logs of deployment. Please follow this document to create an execution through SAP AI Launchpad. At the end, the deployment is running, and the logs indicate that inference server is started.

Step 11: Consume the inference API of model

Finally, we can consume the inference API from step 10. Here is the sample code.

 

import requests, json
from ai_api_client_sdk.ai_api_v2_client import AIAPIV2Client

# Please replace the resource_group if it is not default
resource_group="default" 

# The following configuration comes from the service key of SAP AI Core
ai_api_client = AIAPIV2Client(
    base_url= "<AI_API_URL_FROM_SERVICE_KEY>/v2/lm",
    auth_url= "<URL_FROM_SERVICE_KEY>/oauth/token",
    client_id= "<CLIENT_ID>",
    client_secret="<CLIENT_SECRET>",
    resource_group=resource_group)

token = ai_api_client.rest_client.get_token()
headers = {
        "Authorization": token,
        'ai-resource-group': resource_group,
        "Content-Type": "application/json"}

# prepare the inference base url
deployment_id = "<REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_DEPLOYMENT_ID>"
deployment = ai_api_client.deployment.get(deployment_id)
inference_base_url = f"{deployment.deployment_url}/v1"
headers["Content-Type"] = "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"

predict_endpoint = f"{inference_base_url}/predict"

# predict the book genre with its description
json_data = {
    "Description": "Three Body Problem: A past, present, and future wherein Earth encounters an alien civilization from a nearby system of three Sun-like stars orbiting one another, a representative example of the three-body problem in orbital mechanics."
}

response = requests.post(predict_endpoint, headers=headers, data=json_data)
print('Result:', response.text)

 

 Conclusion

As we have seen, Ludwig simplifies the machine learning process with a low-code approach, enabling business users and data scientists to quickly experiment, build, train, and deploy models. With integration with SAP AI Core, these machine learning operations can be streamlined and managed at scale, fostering faster AI-driven innovation across the enterprise with enterprise-grade of security, scalability and compliance. In the 2nd blog post, we'll replicate the use cases about defect detection with image segmentation and sound-based predictive maintenance with Ludwig with low-code approach, and compare with their original implementation with pro-code approach.

P.S. The publication of the full sample code under github.com/sap-samples is work in process. Please stay tuned.

entry.title: SAP Business AI Cheat Sheet – Your Quick Reference Guide
entry.content:
In this blog, I am going to share my personal cheat sheet related to various AI topics in the context of SAP. This cheat sheet is the collection of most useful blogs, help documents, courses, and more on SAP Business AI, Embedded AI, Joule, Generative AI Hub, HANA vector engine, SAP AI Core etc., which I’ve crafted over time.

Don't forget to bookmark this page for future reference 😉

First, let’s have a quick glimpse on various components and terminologies in SAP Business AI.

 

Note: If you are looking for a simple, step-by-step guide to learn SAP Business AI, you may refer to Generative AI with SAP Blog Series.

If you are new to Generative AI, I will  recommend you to spend few minutes reading the blog series Generative AI for Beginners and 21 Generative AI Jargons Simplified.

 

SAP Business AI

entry.title: Transforming AI Tools into Real-World Solutions on SAP BTP
entry.content:
Capabilities do not always translate into adoption. This is a common oversight when companies consider Generative AI, RAG technologies, and their integration for daily use by employees, partners, or customers.

No Code

No-code solutions are a must for AI technology adoption and utilization in valuable business cases. The ability to create simple implementations without writing a single line of code is essential. By leveraging the integration of Generative AI and RAG, powered by an Action Server with an embedded Conversational AI layer, companies can encapsulate the action server skills of their AI model.

Action Server

The action server is the heart of any AI model, yet it is often overlooked or misplaced. The action server acts as the director, orchestrating interactions with ML models and the vector database. It forms the top layer of the AI model and should include the following components:

  • Action Server
  • Conversational AI
  • Generative AI
  • RAG

AI Model

A conversational AI model serves as the metadata layer for memory-enhanced conversational assistant applications. It is an abstract layer where you define:

  • The skills of the AI assistant, controlled by logical triggers
  • The action groups each skill contains, controlled by logical conditions
  • The action types executed when a skill is triggered

No Code? It Means Application

The application layer of an AI solution must enable AI modeling without coding, allowing users to define groups of skills, triggers, and actions while benefiting from the integration of Generative AI and RAG. Conversational AI plays a key role in deriving the intent of dialogues, acting as a source for logical triggers, and serving as the memory of the conversational assistant.

SAP BTP Services

SAP BTP provides all the necessary components for transforming AI tools into real-world solutions. We recommend the following (inspired by the AI Cookbook):

  • 1 x SAP BTP Cockpit
  • 1 x SAP AI Core
  • 1 x SAP AI Launchpad
  • 1 x SAP HANA Cloud (including Vector Engine)
  • 1 x SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime
  • 1 x SAP BTP, Object Store
  • 1 x SAP Job Scheduling Service
  • 1 x SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC (as the source for SAP-integrated scenarios)

Using these, we developed Skybuffer AI on SAP BTP—a solution that integrates Generative AI and RAG functionality while providing an SAP Fiori interface for AI modeling. After a successful 6-month pilot phase with leading companies in the USA, Europe, and Asia, Skybuffer AI is now market-ready.

Skybuffer AI: No Code, Real-World Solutions on SAP BTP

Skybuffer AI is a set of SAP BTP partner-developed applications that form a ready-to-use platform integrating Generative AI, RAG, and Conversational AI. It enables businesses to automate tasks quickly and efficiently, saving costs, reducing development complexity, and accelerating time to value.

 Fig. 01 Skybuffer AI. Models Overview

Smooth Migration from SAP Conversational AI: Is It Possible?

It took us six years to develop Skybuffer AI into its current form for SAP customers. The journey began with Recast.AI, which was later renamed SAP Conversational AI. Given the development path of Skybuffer AI, we also offer a one-click migration from SAP Conversational AI, ensuring a smooth and efficient transition to the future-ready Skybuffer AI on SAP BTP.

How Does Skybuffer AI Complement SAP Joule?

While SAP Joule functions as an in-application assistant within SAP S/4HANA and SAP Cloud Applications, Skybuffer AI focuses on addressing custom tasks across both SAP and non-SAP systems. It provides flexible integration through APIs and multi-channel support, allowing organizations to model their AI processes. Together, Skybuffer AI and Joule offer a powerful combination, optimizing both standard workflows and custom use cases.

Conclusion

With over 400 SDK-based, backend-integrated Business AI scenarios, Skybuffer AI covers all lines of business in S/4HANA and S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, leveraging SAP BTP AI Core and HANA Cloud services. Follow our article series for insights into Skybuffer AI, or contact us for a demonstration in the comments section.

entry.title: Low-Code Machine Learning with Ludwig and SAP AI Core #2-Defect Detection & Sound Anomaly Detection
entry.content:
In my previous blog post, we have discussed the business values, overview and technical details about end-to-end Low-Code Machine Learning with Ludwig and SAP AI Core. In this blog post, I'll continue sharing my experience of replicating another two custom AI use cases from previous work steam with ludwig in low-code approach, comparing with their original implementations in pro-code approach.

Use Case#2: Defect Detection with Image Segmentation

In this use case, we use computer vision(image segmentation) for defect detection in production line of Light Guide Plate. Please refer to our blog post about Build Your Own Model with TensorFlow in SAP AI Core for defect detection and source code for more details. The original implementation is a U-Net model architecture with Tensorflow by my colleague cesarecalabria.

The original training code is available here with 483 lines of python code. Here is just a screenshot of some code snippet. In order to implement this training code, it not only require you to have a good understanding the unet model architecture, but also the skill of ML development with python and tensorflow.

Okay, no worry. Let's implement the same model based on the same dataset with Ludwig in a much easier way.

Dataset and Data Pre-Process

The original dataset is structured as below. The original images of products are stored in Images folder, their corresponding segmented image are store in Masks folder. Sub folders NG(Defected Product) and OK(Passed) are labels of the product image, which is not in used here.

Every product image in Images folder has its own masked pair image in Masks folder. For example

data/Images/NG/00000_NG_Image.bmpdata/Masks/NG/00000_NG_Mask.bmp
Prdocut ImageMask of Defect

The original images in the dataset has a bmp format, which isn't supported in ludwig image feature, hence, we have to transform the original images from bmp format into jpg. Here is the sample code below.

 

import os
from PIL import Image

def convert_bmp_to_jpg(directory):
    # Walk through all files in the directory and its subdirectories
    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(directory):
        for file in files:
            if file.endswith(".bmp"):
                # Create the full file path
                bmp_path = os.path.join(root, file)

                # Open the BMP image
                with Image.open(bmp_path) as img:
                    # Convert the image to RGB mode
                    img = img.convert("RGB")

                    # Create the new file name with .jpg extension
                    jpg_path = os.path.splitext(bmp_path)[0] + ".jpg"

                    # Save the image as a JPG
                    img.save(jpg_path, "JPEG")

                # Optionally, delete the original BMP file after conversion
                os.remove(bmp_path)

                print(f"Converted {bmp_path} to {jpg_path}")

# Replace './data' with the actual path to your directory
convert_bmp_to_jpg("./data")

 

 In addition, we'll also need to create a csv file as dataset.csv based on the image dataset like

 

image_path,mask_path,label
data/Images/NG/00000_NG_Image.jpg,data/Masks/NG/00000_NG_Mask.jpg,NG
data/Images/NG/00001_NG_Image.jpg,data/Masks/NG/00001_NG_Mask.jpg,NG

 

Here is the python script to generate dataset.csv based on the folder structure of dataset

 

import os
import csv

# Define paths
image_dir = "data/Images"
mask_dir = "data/Masks"
output_csv = "dataset.csv"

# Collect all image and mask file paths
image_files = {"NG": [], "OK": []}
mask_files = {"NG": [], "OK": []}

for label in ["NG", "OK"]:
    image_files[label] = sorted([os.path.join(image_dir, label, f) for f in os.listdir(os.path.join(image_dir, label))])
    mask_files[label] = sorted([os.path.join(mask_dir, label, f) for f in os.listdir(os.path.join(mask_dir, label))])

# Create and write to the CSV file
with open(output_csv, mode="w", newline="") as file:
    writer = csv.writer(file)
    writer.writerow(["image", "mask", "label"])

    for label in ["NG", "OK"]:
        for img, mask in zip(image_files[label], mask_files[label]):
            writer.writerow([img, mask, label])

print(f"Dataset CSV file has been created: {output_csv}")

 

 

A declarative config.yaml

Based on the dataset above, all the code we need to train a defect detection model of LPG product with image segmentation is a declarative config.yaml as below. Please refer to this for more details about image feature in ludwig. Literally, we have simplified the training from 480 lines of python code into 40 lines of declarative yaml code. It is way too easy and straight-forward, even application developer and business users with limited machine learning knowledge can write such a declarative config.yam. Having said that, you still need the skill of data pre-processing, and the knowledge of selecting the input and output features based on business objectives and dataset.

 

input_features:
  - name: image_path
    type: image
    preprocessing:
      num_processes: 6
      height: 224
      width: 224
      infer_image_max_height: 1024
      infer_image_max_width: 1024
    encoder: unet

output_features:
  - name: mask_path
    type: image
    preprocessing:
      num_processes: 6
      height: 224
      width: 224
      infer_image_max_height: 1024
      infer_image_max_width: 1024
      infer_image_num_classes: true
      num_channels: 1
      num_classes: 2
    decoder:
      type: unet
      num_fc_layers: 0
    loss:
      type: softmax_cross_entropy
    metric:
      type: iou

combiner:
  type: concat
  num_fc_layers: 0

trainer:
  epochs: 100
  early_stop: -1
  batch_size: 1
  max_batch_size: 1

 

For the model training and serving, you can just use the ludwig command lines, like ludwig experiment/serve etc. which we have gone through in last blog post in detail. So are running the same ludwig workloads within SAP AI Core.

Pro-Code VS Low-Code

Next, let's have a look another use case about audio features.

Use Case#3: Predictive Maintenance based on Machinery Sound Anomaly Detection

In the last use case, we'll detect the sound anomaly of machinery for predictive maintenance of production line. Please refer to our blog post about Sound-based predictive maintenance with SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad and source code for more details. The original implementation is a custom CNN model with Tensorflow by my colleague amagnani.

The original training code is available here with 345 lines of python code. Here is just a screenshot of some code snippet. In order to implement this training code, it not only require you to have a good understanding of CNN, but also the skill of ML development with python and tensorflow.

Okay, no worry. Let's implement the same model based on the same dataset with Ludwig in a much easier way.

Dataset and Data Pre-Process

The original dataset is structured as below. The sub folders anomaly1, anomaly2 and ok are the labels of the audio samples.

We'll also need to create a csv file as dataset.csv based on the image dataset like 

 

audio_path,class
data/ok/clip0568.wav,ok
data/anomaly1/clip0583.wav,anomaly1
data/anomaly2/clip0597.wav,anomaly2

 

Here is the python script to generate dataset.csv based on the folder structure of dataset 

 

import os
import csv

# Define the root directory and the output CSV file
root_dir = "data"
output_csv = "dataset.csv"

# Prepare the data list
data = []

# Traverse the directory structure
for subdir, dirs, files in os.walk(root_dir):
    for file in files:
        if file.endswith(".wav"):  # Filter for .wav files
            # Construct the file path and class label
            file_path = os.path.join(subdir, file)
            class_label = os.path.basename(subdir)
            data.append([file_path, class_label])

# Write to the CSV file
with open(output_csv, "w", newline="") as csvfile:
    writer = csv.writer(csvfile)
    writer.writerow(["audio_path", "class"])  # Write header
    writer.writerows(data)

print(f"CSV file '{output_csv}' generated successfully.")

 

A declarative config.yaml

Based on the dataset above, all the code we need to train a sound anomaly detection with CNN is a declarative config.yaml as below. Please refer to this for more details about image feature in ludwig. Literally, we have simplified the training from 345 lines of python code into 19 lines of declarative yaml code. It is much easier and more straight-forward, even application developers and business users with limited machine learning knowledge can write such a declarative config.yam. Having said that, you still need the skill of data pre-processing, and the knowledge of selecting the input and output features based on business objectives and dataset.

 

input_features:
  - name: audio_path
    type: audio
    preprocessing:
      audio_file_length_limit_in_s: 7.5
      audio_feature:
        type: stft
        window_length_in_s: 0.04
        window_shift_in_s: 0.02
    encoder:
      type: parallel_cnn

output_features:
  - name: class
    type: category

trainer:
  epochs: 100
  early_stop: -1

 

Pro-Code VS Low-Code

Conclusion

In these two use cases, we have witnessed the ease and efficiency of the low-code machine learning with Ludwig and SAP AI Core over the pro-code approach. Ludwig simplifies the machine learning process with a low-code approach. Not only opening the door of machine learning for citizen ML developers like application developer, even business user etc. but also increasing the productivity of the professional machine learning engineers and data scientists to quickly experiment, build, train, and deploy models. When paired with SAP AI Core, these machine learning operations can be streamlined and managed at scale, fostering faster AI-driven innovation across the enterprise with enterprise-grade of security, scalability and compliance.

entry.title: Where can I start learning how to use SAP's generative AI Hub to solve your business problems?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Solving Your Business Problems Using Prompts and LLMs in SAP's Generative AI Hub learning journey

Overview
At the end of this learning journey, you will be able to use SAP's generative AI Hub to solve your business problems using LLMs, SAP AI Launchpad, and generative-ai-hub SDK. 
You will be able to apply techniques for refining and evaluating prompts using different large language models in generative AI hub.

Learning objectives

  • Describe AI foundation and generative AI hub 
  • Learn how to access SAP's generative AI hub.
  • Identify the need of using SDKs for LLMs.
  • Describe SAP's Generative AI hub SDK.
  • Explain  advanced prompting methods.
  • Describe different models in generative AI hub.
  • Configure different models in generative AI hub.
  • Explain generative AI applications beyond chatbots, using software-driven input and output processing scenarios.

Prerequisites

  • Navigating Large Language Models fundamentals and techniques for your use case

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: 🚀Fire of Innovations: Highlights from SAP TechEd 2024 📢
entry.content:
The SAP TechEd 2024 event, themed “Fire of Innovations,” has once again set the stage for groundbreaking advancements in enterprise technology. Held virtually on October 8-9, this year’s conference showcased a plethora of new tools, updates, and strategies designed to empower businesses and developers alike. Here’s a deep dive into some of the most exciting announcements and innovations from the event.

1. Joule: The AI Copilot Revolution

One of the standout highlights was the unveiling of new capabilities for Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot. Joule has been supercharged with collaborative AI agents that can perform complex, cross-disciplinary tasks. These agents are designed to break down silos within organizations, enhancing productivity and allowing human workers to focus on more creative and strategic activities.

For instance, in dispute management, autonomous AI agents can now analyze and resolve issues such as incorrect invoices or duplicate payments. In financial accounting, these agents streamline processes like bill payments and ledger updates, quickly addressing any inconsistencies.

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Enhancements

SAP continues to expand its Business Technology Platform (BTP), which is central to its cloud strategy. This year, SAP announced the expansion of BTP coverage through eight new SAP-managed data centers. Additionally, the SAP Discovery Center now offers a Quick-Account-Setup feature, enabling businesses to deploy specific use cases with just a click.

The integration of ABAP Cloud into SAP Build was another significant update. This integration allows developers to create and maintain ABAP Cloud projects directly from the SAP Build lobby, ensuring a seamless and efficient development process.

3. Innovations in SAP Build

SAP Build has been strengthened to support a “Clean Core” strategy, which is crucial for maintaining system consistency and upgradeability. The SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, introduced for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, helps both key users and developers create on-stack and side-by-side extensions in an embedded experience.

4. Harnessing the Power of Data with SAP Knowledge Graph

SAP introduced the SAP Knowledge Graph, a next-generation solution that connects SAP data with rich business context. This tool is poised to help developers unlock the full value of SAP data, driving more informed decision-making and innovation.

5. Global Reach and Accessibility

This year’s SAP TechEd was a truly global event, with a free two-day virtual format that included keynotes, technical sessions, and developer enablement activities. The event was designed to be accessible to a worldwide audience, ensuring that everyone could benefit from the latest SAP innovations.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 has once again demonstrated SAP’s commitment to driving innovation and empowering businesses with cutting-edge technology. From AI advancements with Joule to enhancements in SAP BTP and SAP Build, the “Fire of Innovations” theme truly encapsulates the transformative potential of these new tools and strategies. As businesses continue to navigate the complexities of the digital age, SAP’s innovations provide a robust foundation for growth and success.

Stay tuned for more updates and insights from SAP TechEd as we continue to explore the future of enterprise technology.

Feel free to share your thoughts or ask any questions about the event! What innovation are you most excited about?

entry.title: SAP Developer News: TechEd Day 1
entry.content:
Description

  • AI Sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog?tab.day=20241009&search.track=1692371026432002VTW0

AI Innovations – Part 2

  • Artificial Intelligence News: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/ai.html
  • AI Sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog?tab.day=20241009&search.track=1692371026432002VTW0

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 TechEd Intro
1:09 Build Code Enhancements for Developers
3:30 AI Innovations – Part 1
4:33 AI Innovations – Part 2

Transcript

[Intro] Well, a developer in his houseboat, innovating cutting-edge tech from the high seas. He could have kept his anchors away, but he didn't.

[DJ] So while the crew were at lunch we've snuck into the studios to record this item on the couch. So we're at TechEd as we can see here I've got some a card here and one of the items announced at TechEd as you may know is that there are new capabilities that have been added and will be added to SAP Build the complete solution for building end-to-end extensions on SAP S/4 HANA Cloud and on other line of business applications. There are three here written down that I want to highlight.

There's a new extension wizard and it allows you to jump directly from SAP S/4 HANA Cloud public edition straight to SAP Build. Why is that important? Because there's a lot of context within the context of the application and that context objects events and so on business data is super important not to lose that so this new extension wizard which is available for building side-by-side and on stack extensions allows that jump including the context there are new generative AI capabilities with dual coming in this quarter for example alongside the already existing code generation facilities, there are or there will be code explanation facilities and also document search. I think they're great complements to what already exists.

On the low code arena, there are also dual enhancements to help with the creation of UIs and workflow automation.

And last but not least, there's now direct access or there will be, by the end of this year, direct access from SAP Build to ABAP development environments in the cloud and on BTP. So basically the whole idea is that this is one step closer to a more unified development environment. You can jump from the lobby to SAP Build Code, SAP Build Apps and now to specific ABAP development environments and this helps enormously with success for teams especially in fusion development contexts. Anyway check out the announcements from SAP TechEd for more details on these new SAP build capabilities.

[Nora] There's a lot happening with AI at TechEd at the moment and you've heard a lot of news and I'm just going to try to put as much as possible into the next couple of minutes. So there has been a lot of rumor on the SAP Foundation model for a while now and it's getting real and if you want to know more about the SAP Foundation model you should definitely check out the session AI 102 on Wednesday. SAP Foundation Model and Knowledge Graph, where you can learn about the SAP Foundation Model and how it can actually interpret structured data and fill in missing data points. Then we also have a lot of new features in the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core. Mainly we have new models coming up, so we will have the IBM Granite model series. We will also have the Dali 3 model and the Amazon Titan image generator model. Those will all be available until the end of 2024. We also have other new features of Generative AI Hub, for example, the orchestration capability, including prompt templates and data masking. And then we also have some new features with Joule and Kevin is going to tell you more about that.

[Kevin] SAP is going to expand and elevate Joule's capabilities to drive business innovation by providing SAP Joule Studio to create custom skills for Joule, but it also will integrate Joule with SAP HANA Cloud to help you generate code for SAP HANA Cloud and help you work easier with SAP HANA Cloud as well. The SAP Cloud SDK for AI was available for Python already, but now we got that for Java, JavaScript, and ABAP, helping you to integrate the capabilities of SAP Generative AI Hub of SAP AI Core directly into your business enterprise applications.

entry.title: Accelerate the Budget Approval Process with SAP HANA Cloud vector engine
entry.content:
About Sierra’s OhZone SBF:

Sierra has introduced a tailored BTP application known as SBF – Smart Budgeting and Forecasting. The solution  is recently SAP ICC certified too. Have a look at it in the Certified Solutions Directory. The innovative tool streamlines the entire budgeting process, from the initial request through to final approval. With the ability to create dynamic form layouts and manage workflows, SBF enhances budgeting efficiency. Integrated with SAC – Planning, it supports detailed planning and facilitates team collaboration. Users can also export data to Excel and attach it as supporting documentation to their budget requests.

          Pic 1: Process Workflow

  • Multiple budget form requests and workflows can be handled with the process in an effective and seamless way.
  • Tailored for Budgeting Team, this solution transforms budgeting and forecasting processes, offering a seamless, AI-driven approach for managing both capital and operational budgets across departments.
  • It streamlines every phase, from initiation to final approval, enabling predictive insights for smarter decision-making.
  • This all-in-one platform represents the future of efficient, strategic financial planning.

How has Gen AI and SAP HANA Cloud Vector enhanced OhZone SBF?

Case 1: Gen AI

The form layouts for different organizations and even for different user groups within the same organization vary widely. We have a low-code no-code, drag and drop custom form builder where a super user can create the forms and publish them for users.

Though we have this low-code no-code, dynamic form builder, still the super user needs to create multiple budget request form layouts depending on different departments or different user groups.

Gen AI – Solution Enhancement

Sierra has used the Gen AI API from within the SBF application to create the first draft of the form by just entering a prompt. We have used the GPT 4.0 model in the SAP AI Core.

Form Generation:

  • Leverage the  Large Language Model (LLM) for form generation. The context is initialized with form component building blocks, and based on the user's prompt, the form is generated using  LLM.
  • The process starts with setting up the integration between the systems to ensure smooth communication and data exchange.
  • User requirements and templates are provided as input for Gen AI to generate the form structure. The response JSON from LLM is then processed and rendered as a form for the end user

 

Watch a demo here on how Sierra has leveraged the SAP generative AI hub to generate budget request forms in its SBF application.

 

Demo Video Link: https://youtu.be/Pv9iZV5zDMY  

 

Pic 2: Dynamic Form Builder Prompt

 

 

Pic 3: Dynamic Form Builder Prompt Result

 

Value Proposition for customers

By prompting, SAP Generative AI Hub helps users simply and quickly create custom, dynamic budget request form layouts. They experience a significantly improved and automated approach to designing multiple form layouts. Our solution streamlines the process, enabling faster and more efficient creation of diverse forms with ease. This enhancement not only saves time but also ensures accuracy and consistency across all your form layouts

Case 2: SAP HANA Cloud Vector

While initiating and filling the budget request form, the requestors are used to attaching large PDFs, excel files, word documents as a substantiating documents like Policies , Union Collective bargaining agreements , Government Orders or policies, Market data etc. The Approver has to read all this document which is a time-consuming process and makes it difficult to take a decision in a short time .

 

SAP HANA Cloud vector – Solution Enhancement

Summarization of long documents and querying using Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)with the  SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine enables quicker understanding of the attachments to budget request forms and allows taking informed decisions while approving the budget requests.​ This and eases the process of Budget approvals significantly. Initially, it took 30 minutes to create the form for reading, but now the process takes only 5 minutes, resulting in a 25-minute time savings.

 

    SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine:

  • Different unstructured documents like Budget Manuals are converted to Vector embeddings using an embedding model.
  • We have used text-embedding-ada-002 from Azure Open AI Foundational models in the SAP Gen AI Hub to convert the text into embeddings.
  • Vector embeddings are stored in the SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database

 

Demo Video Link: https://youtu.be/8Qk5yMcyLeE

Pic 4: HANA Cloud Vector

Value Proposition for Customers

Our solution dramatically accelerates the budget approval process by expediting the review of attached documents. It allows for simultaneous querying and analysis of multiple documents using natural language. This remarkably streamlines the review workflow. The gained efficiency not only speeds up the approval timeline but also ensures a thorough and organized examination of all relevant materials, enhancing overall .

In a short period of time, more approvals can be completed as the approver can process documents 70% faster, significantly reducing the overall time required.

 

 

High-Level Process Flow:

  • The architecture connects different SAP services and systems (S/4HANA, HANA Cloud, Analytics Cloud) in a secure and AI-augmented environment, ensuring seamless data flow and integration.
  • Application Clients, access services through the Launchpad Service, authenticated by the Identity Authentication Service.
  • Application data processing and storage aspects, that interact with SAP HANA Cloud include SAP BTP Apps, which run on Node.js and HDI Container.
  • Secure communication between the SAP BTP and SAP S/4HANA is facilitated by the Cloud Connector, using HTTP OData over a secure tunnel.
  • Data from SAP S/4HANA can be imported into SAP Analytics Cloud to provide analytics and reporting functionalities. It has the integrated tool Just Ask, which could involve natural language queries, indicating AI-driven insights.

 

Pic 5: SBF Architecture

 

For Additional Information:

  • SBF ICC Certificate
  • SBF SAP Store list

entry.title: IBM Granite.13b.chat Large Language Model- Now GA on the SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
SAP’s generative AI Hub is the central place for BTP application developers to access and use generative AI tools and models, and developers can now choose IBM Granite as their large language model (LLM). This article will explain some of Granite’s unique characteristics and then show you how to access and use it.

IBM Granite.13b.chat is a decoder, English-only, enterprise-grade LLM and part of a series of IBM foundation models for generative artificial intelligence tasks. IBM stands behind Granite LLMs with IP indemnification protection according to its Standard Terms and Conditions, to provide users with peace of mind. Granite.13b.chat was trained on enterprise-relevant content that meets rigorous data governance, regulatory, and risk criteria defined and enforced by the IBM AI Ethics Code and Chief Privacy Office. You can read the details here: https://www.ibm.com/downloads/cas/X9W4O6BM. Granite.13b.chat delivers high accuracy for retrieval augmented generation (RAG), summarization, content generation, named entity recognition, insight extraction, and classification.

A considerable portion of Granite training data is from business domains. For business-specific tasks in domains such as finance, some IBM performance benchmarks suggest that the accuracy of Granite models is on par with larger models (70B parameter+) at lower latency and, due to its smaller size (13B parameter), lower infrastructure spending.

AI capabilities in SAP AI Core & SAP AI Launchpad that utilize the generative AI hub can leverage Granite.13b.chat.

Get started by:

  1. Learning how to use foundational models on SAP AI Core (https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-consumption-llm.html), and
  2. learning how to prompt Granite.13b.chat (https://dataplatform.cloud.ibm.com/docs/content/wsj/analyze-data/fm-models-ibm-chat.html?context=wx&audience=wdp).

For more information about Granite.13b.chat and instruction-tuning details, see the model card (https://dataplatform.cloud.ibm.com/login?context=wx&sign_in_target=%2Fwx%2Fsamples%2Fmodels%2Fibm%2Fgranite-13b-chat-v2%3Fcontext%3Dwx%3Fcontext%3Dwx%26audience%3Dwdp).

entry.title: Introducing the SAP Cloud SDK for AI (JavaScript/TypeScript) 🎉
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the initial release of the SAP Cloud SDK for AI.
Integrate generative AI capabilities into your SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) applications and leverage the Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Core.
 
If you’re developing on SAP BTP, the SAP Cloud SDK for AI is designed to make AI integration more accessible and impactful in your applications. With features that streamline deployment management, enhance content safety, and simplify model orchestration, this SDK allows you to quickly incorporate advanced AI capabilities without extensive setup. Whether you need flexible workflows, secure data handling, or seamless integration with generative models, this SDK offers powerful tools to bring AI-driven functionality to your SAP BTP solutions.
 
In this blog post, we will introduce you to the key packages and their features.

AI Orchestration with @​​​​​sap-ai-sdk/orchestration

 
Leverage the generative AI Hub orchestration service and configure templating, content filtering, and data masking for your applications with the @sap-ai-sdk/orchestration package.
With the orchestration service, you can streamline AI interactions and ensure compliance with content safety guidelines.
  • Templating: Create dynamic prompts with placeholders to customize AI interactions based on user inputs.
  • Content Filtering: Apply filters to ensure input and output adhere to content safety guidelines.
  • Data Masking: Anonymize and pseudonymize sensitive information.
  • Grounding: Integrate external data sources to provide contextually relevant information (planned for Q4 2024).

AI Management with @​​​​​​​​sap-ai-sdk/ai-api

 
Automate processes such as creating artifacts, configurations, and deployments, executing batch inference jobs, as well as managing Docker registries and object storage for training data.
The @sap-ai-sdk/ai-api package provides comprehensive tools for managing scenarios and workflows in SAP AI Core.
  • Artifact Management: Register and manage datasets and other model artifacts.
  • Configuration Management: Set up configurations for different models and use cases.
  • Deployment Management: Deploy AI models and manage their lifecycle within SAP AI Core.

LangChain Integration with @​​​​sap-ai-sdk/langchai

 
The @sap-ai-sdk/langchain package provides LangChain-compatible clients for Azure OpenAI models deployed in SAP AI Core, enabling sophisticated AI pipelines within your SAP BTP applications.
  • Seamless Integration: Built on SAP Cloud SDK for AI's foundation model clients, ensuring compatibility with SAP AI Core and LangChain's ecosystem.
  • Flexible Clients: Easily initialize chat and embedding clients with support for advanced features like templating and output parsing.
  • RAG Support: Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation workflows by combining embedding capabilities with LangChain's text splitters and vector stores.

Generative AI with @​​​​​​​​​​​​sap-ai-sdk/foundation-models

 
The @sap-ai-sdk/foundation-models package offers streamlined access to specific generative AI models accessible via Generative AI Hub.
It provides a more focused interface compared to the full orchestration capabilities, concentrating on direct model interactions.

This package is ideal for developers who need direct access to foundation models for inference and embedding requests without the additional layers of templating, content filtering, or data masking provided by the orchestration service.

Getting Started

 
You will need Node v20 or higher and an ESM (ECMAScript Modules) compatible application to use the packages. 
To explore these packages further, check out our sample code, which shows the usage of the various SDK packages.

Support and Feedback

 
We value your feedback on this initial release! 
If you need support or want to share your thoughts and ideas, go ahead and open an issue on GitHub.

Latest News

  • Visit sap.com/ai and explore our portfolio
  • Explore the available AI capabilities on SAP Discovery Center.
  • Discover the latest announcements in the SAP TechEd Press Release and the SAP TechEd News Guide.
  • Review the SAP Road Map Explorer for a detailed view of upcoming product innovations.
  • Join the SAP Community page to connect with experts and share knowledge.
